title: The virtuous cycle of workspace structure
author: Philip Fulcher

entities.name: Nx
entities.description: A build system and monorepo management tool that provides specialized tooling for organizing and maintaining workspace structure

entities.name: Nx Enterprise
entities.description: Enterprise support program offered by Nx for companies implementing monorepo architectures

entities.name: Architecture
entities.description: Software architecture patterns and approaches for organizing code and systems

entities.name: Domain Driven Design
entities.description: An architectural approach that can enhance modulith architecture by organizing code around business domains

concepts.name: Modulith Architecture
concepts.description: An architecture pattern that sits between monolith and microservices, maintaining a single deployable while breaking code into separate, maintainable parts

concepts.name: Hexagonal Architecture
concepts.description: An architectural pattern that can be applied to enhance modulith architecture by organizing code around ports and adapters

concepts.name: Workspace Structure Goals
concepts.description: Three core goals for organizing workspaces: projects with well-defined purpose, quick code location, and co-locating frequently updated code

concepts.name: Project Types
concepts.description: Four primary library types in Nx workspaces: feature libraries (container components), UI libraries (presentational), data-access libraries (backend interaction), and utility libraries (low-level utilities)

concepts.name: Scope-Type-Identifier Naming
concepts.description: A naming convention pattern that combines scope, type, and identifier to create self-documenting project names (e.g., products-feature-details)

concepts.name: Module Boundary Rules
concepts.description: Enforcement rules that define which project types can depend on which other types, maintaining separation of concerns

concepts.name: Virtuous Cycle
concepts.description: A positive feedback loop where well-defined project purposes lead to good naming, which enables quick code location, which results in changes in the right places, reinforcing the structure

concepts.name: Dump Projects
concepts.description: Anti-pattern projects (like 'util' or 'components') that accumulate code when developers can't determine proper placement, leading to tech debt

concepts.name: Tag-Based Enforcement
concepts.description: Using tags (scope and type) on projects to automatically enforce module boundary rules and maintain workspace structure

codeBlocks.content:
libs/
  products/               <---- grouped by scope
    feature-details/      <---- project prefixed with type

  check-out/              <---- grouped by scope
    feature-cart/         <---- project prefixed with type
    data-access-taxes/    <---- project prefixed with type

  shared/                 <---- grouped by scope
    ui-forms/             <---- project prefixed with type
    util-dates            <---- project prefixed with type
codeBlocks.description: Example workspace structure showing scope-based grouping with type-prefixed projects
